question_text:
GOVERNMENT OF INDIA
 MINISTRY OF PERSONNEL, PUBLIC GRIEVANCES AND PENSIONS
 (DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL & TRAINING)

 LOK SABHA
 UNSTARRED QUESTION NO. 269
 (
answer_text:
24.07.2024)

 RESERVATION FOR SCs/STs/OBCs

269 SHRI TANUJ PUNIA:

 Will the PRIME MINISTER be pleased to state:

(a) the proportion of population of persons belonging to the SC, ST and OBC communities
 reserved for Central Services, State/UT-wise for the year 2021-22; and
(b) the details of the number of posts lying vacant, Ministry/Department-wise and posts
 notified by the Government under various Departments/Ministries?

 ANSWER

MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PERSONNEL, PUBLIC GRIEVANCES
AND PENSIONS AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE PRIME MINISTER’S OFFICE
 (DR. JITENDRA SINGH)

 (a) & (b): Reservation in posts and services under the Central Government is provided to SC, ST
 and OBC communities @ 15%, 7.5% and 27% respectively on direct recruitment on all India
 basis by open competition. In the matter of promotion, the reservation is provided to SC and ST
 @ 15% and 7.5% respectively.
 Occurrence and filling of vacancies is a continuous process. DoPT has issued instructions to the
 Ministries/Departments of the Central Government to designate an officer of the rank of Deputy
 Secretary and above, as Liaison Officer, to ensure due compliance of the orders and instructions
 pertaining to reservation. Further, each Ministry/Department is required to set up a Special
 Reservation Cell under the direct control of the Liaison Officer to assist her/him in discharge of
 duties.

 *******
answer_body:
(a) & (b): Reservation in posts and services under the Central Government is provided to SC, ST
 and OBC communities @ 15%, 7.5% and 27% respectively on direct recruitment on all India
 basis by open competition. In the matter of promotion, the reservation is provided to SC and ST
 @ 15% and 7.5% respectively.
 Occurrence and filling of vacancies is a continuous process. DoPT has issued instructions to the
 Ministries/Departments of the Central Government to designate an officer of the rank of Deputy
 Secretary and above, as Liaison Officer, to ensure due compliance of the orders and instructions
 pertaining to reservation. Further, each Ministry/Department is required to set up a Special
 Reservation Cell under the direct control of the Liaison Officer to assist her/him in discharge of
 duties.

 *******

question_text:
GOVERNMENT OF INDIA
 MINISTRY OF PERSONNEL, PUBLIC GRIEVANCES AND PENSIONS
 (DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL & TRAINING)

 LOK SABHA
 UNSTARRED QUESTION NO. 285
 (
answer_text:
24.07.2024)

 IAS/IPS/IRS OFFICERS

†285 SHRI BHAUSAHEB RAJARAM WAKCHAURE:

 Will the PRIME MINISTER be pleased to state:

(a) the total number of IAS, IPS and IFS officers belonging to SC/ST/OBC communities as on
 date;
(b) whether the representation has not been given as per the proportion to their population; and
(c) if so, the details thereof and the reasons therefor?

 ANSWER

MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PERSONNEL, PUBLIC GRIEVANCES
AND PENSIONS AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE PRIME MINISTER’S OFFICE
 (DR. JITENDRA SINGH)

(a) to (c): Recruitment to the Indian Administrative Service (IAS), Indian Police Service (IPS) and
Indian Forest Service (IFS) are done through Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) in
accordance with the relevant Rules. As per extant instructions, reservation is provided to
Scheduled Castes (SCs), Scheduled Tribes (STs) and Other Backward Classes (OBCs) at the rate
of 15%, 7.5% and 27% respectively in recruitment of IAS, IPS and IFS. Year-wise and category-
wise number of persons belonging to SCs, STs and OBCs appointed to the IAS, IPS and IFS as
Direct Recruit during the last five years, are as under: -

 IAS IPS IFS
 Year OBC SC ST OBC SC ST OBC SC ST

 2018 54 29 14 40 23 09 40 16 08
 2019 61 28 14 42 24 09 33 13 07
 2020 61 25 13 41 23 10 31 13 06
 2021 54 30 13 57 28 14 34 13 07
 2022 58 28 14 49 25 20 40 16 08
 *******
answer_body:
(a) to (c): Recruitment to the Indian Administrative Service (IAS), Indian Police Service (IPS) and
Indian Forest Service (IFS) are done through Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) in
accordance with the relevant Rules. As per extant instructions, reservation is provided to
Scheduled Castes (SCs), Scheduled Tribes (STs) and Other Backward Classes (OBCs) at the rate
of 15%, 7.5% and 27% respectively in recruitment of IAS, IPS and IFS. Year-wise and category-
wise number of persons belonging to SCs, STs and OBCs appointed to the IAS, IPS and IFS as
Direct Recruit during the last five years, are as under: -

 IAS IPS IFS
 Year OBC SC ST OBC SC ST OBC SC ST

 2018 54 29 14 40 23 09 40 16 08
 2019 61 28 14 42 24 09 33 13 07
 2020 61 25 13 41 23 10 31 13 06
 2021 54 30 13 57 28 14 34 13 07
 2022 58 28 14 49 25 20 40 16 08
 *******

question_text:
GOVERNMENT OF INDIA
 MINISTRY OF PERSONNEL, PUBLIC GRIEVANCES AND PENSIONS
 (DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL & TRAINING)

 LOK SABHA
 UNSTARRED QUESTION NO. 257
 (
answer_text:
27.11.2024)

 LIAISON OFFICER TO FILL UP VACANT POSTS RESERVED FOR SCs/STs/OBCs

†257. SHRI DHARMENDRA YADAV:

Will the PRIME MINISTER be pleased to state:

(a) whether a Liaision Officer is appointed in each Ministry/Department of the Union Government
and subordinate organisations to fill up the vacant posts reserved for Scheduled Castes /Scheduled
Tribes/Other Backward Classes to ensure proper compliance of orders and instructions regarding
filling up of reserved vacancies and if so, the details thereof;
(b) if not, the reasons therefor and the names of the Ministries/Departments /Organisations /
Public Sector Undertakings in which Liaison Officers have not been appointed to safeguard the
interests of Scheduled Castes/Scheduled Tribes/Other Backward Classes; and
(c) the steps taken by the Government in this regard?

 ANSWER

MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PERSONNEL, PUBLIC GRIEVANCES
AND PENSIONS AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE PRIME MINISTER’S OFFICE
 (DR. JITENDRA SINGH)

(a) to (c): As per the instructions issued by DoPT, each Ministry/ Department is required to appoint
an officer, not below the rank of Deputy Secretary, as Liaison Officer for SC/ST and OBC, in
order to ensure compliance of the orders and instructions pertaining to reservation of vacancies in
favour of backward classes. All the offices and organizations under the Ministry/ Departments are
required to designate a Liaison Officer in the same manner as required in the Ministry/Department
to ensure implementation of reservation policies. However, the overall responsibility for
implementation of the reservation policy in appointments lies with the Liaison Officer of the
administrative Ministry/Department concerned.

 As per the information received, all the Ministries/Departments have appointed Liaison
Officers for SC/ST/OBC.

 *******
answer_body:
(a) to (c): As per the instructions issued by DoPT, each Ministry/ Department is required to appoint
an officer, not below the rank of Deputy Secretary, as Liaison Officer for SC/ST and OBC, in
order to ensure compliance of the orders and instructions pertaining to reservation of vacancies in
favour of backward classes. All the offices and organizations under the Ministry/ Departments are
required to designate a Liaison Officer in the same manner as required in the Ministry/Department
to ensure implementation of reservation policies. However, the overall responsibility for
implementation of the reservation policy in appointments lies with the Liaison Officer of the
administrative Ministry/Department concerned.

 As per the information received, all the Ministries/Departments have appointed Liaison
Officers for SC/ST/OBC.

 *******
